description: Target claims for NS enstrophy bound discovery. These are genuinely open questions.

targets.context: The tightest possible bound on 3D enstrophy growth rate dOmega/dt in terms of known quantities is:
candidates: dOmega/dt <= C * Omega^(3/2) * E^(-1/2) — enstrophy growth is controlled by the ratio of enstrophy to energy, giving a conditional regularity criterion
dOmega/dt <= C * Omega * ||omega||_infinity — linear in enstrophy times the vorticity maximum, reproducing the BKM condition
dOmega/dt <= C * Omega^2 / E — quadratic in enstrophy divided by energy, implying blowup only when enstrophy vastly exceeds energy
dOmega/dt <= C * P^(2/3) * Omega^(1/3) — involving palinstrophy, connecting enstrophy growth to its own dissipation rate
targets.notes: Candidate 0 is related to the Ladyzhenskaya inequality. Candidate 3 connects enstrophy growth to palinstrophy which is novel.

targets.context: If an approximate invariant exists that constrains enstrophy growth in 3D Navier-Stokes, its most likely form is:
candidates: a weighted integral involving both |omega|^2 and the alignment angle between omega and the strain eigenvectors, penalizing configurations prone to stretching
a ratio like H^2 / (E * Omega) combining helicity, energy, and enstrophy whose boundedness implies regularity
a functional of the vorticity direction field alone (not magnitude), since geometric regularity criteria show direction controls blowup
a Lyapunov functional that decreases along NS trajectories and whose positivity prevents enstrophy blowup
targets.notes: Candidate 0 combines magnitude and geometry. Candidate 1 is a dimensionless ratio of known quantities. Candidate 2 builds on Constantin-Fefferman. Candidate 3 is a classical stability approach.

targets.context: The depletion of nonlinearity (vorticity aligning with intermediate strain eigenvector) persists in 3D turbulence because:
candidates: it is a consequence of the pressure Hessian: the pressure field dynamically adjusts to suppress the most dangerous vortex-strain alignments
local energy conservation forces vorticity toward the intermediate eigenvector since alignment with the stretching eigenvector would violate the energy budget
it is a statistical artifact of turbulence averaging — individual flow regions can have arbitrary alignment, but the mean is depleted
the restricted Euler dynamics (ignoring pressure and viscosity) naturally evolves toward intermediate alignment as a dynamical attractor
targets.notes: Candidate 0 (pressure Hessian) is the leading theory. Candidate 3 (restricted Euler) has been studied but is incomplete. Candidate 1 is novel.

targets.context: The quantity whose boundedness is MOST LIKELY to be equivalent to 3D Navier-Stokes regularity (beyond the known BKM vorticity condition) is:
candidates: the L^3 norm of velocity ||u||_3 — the critical norm under NS scaling, where the borderline case remains the key open problem
the L^(3/2) norm of pressure ||p||_{3/2} — pressure encodes global information and its blowup would imply velocity blowup
the L^infinity norm of the vorticity direction xi = omega/|omega| gradient — geometric regularity controls blowup
a mixed norm involving both velocity and vorticity: ||u||_3 * ||omega||_3 / ||u||_6^2 — a dimensionless combination at the critical scaling
targets.notes: Candidate 0 is the Escauriaza-Seregin-Sverak endpoint. Candidate 2 is Constantin-Fefferman. Candidate 3 is novel.
